summary: Decompiled most of the common fighter Warp Star action file by renaming `ftCo_WarpStarJump` to the broader `ftCo_WarpStar`, moving Warp Star fall callbacks out of `asm/melee/ft/ft_0C31.s`, and adding C implementations or C-equivalent WIP bodies for the fall anim/IASA/phys/coll/cam path. The PR report claims 96.72% file match across 1.83 KB, with all listed functions at 100% except `ftCo_800C4C60` at 87.03% and `ftCo_800C4724` at 99.87%. It also improved supporting types/prototypes, including typed `ftCommonData` floats, a real `ftCollisionBox` in Warp Star motion variables, and typed item/collision helper signatures.
what_changed: Renamed `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_WarpStarJump.c` to `ftCo_WarpStar.c` and `ftCo_WarpStarJump.h` to `ftCo_WarpStar.h`, then updated includes in `ftCo_Init.c`, `ftCo_ItemGet.c`, and `obj_files.mk`.

Removed the assembly definitions for `ftCo_WarpStarFall_Anim`, `ftCo_WarpStarFall_IASA`, `ftCo_WarpStarFall_Phys`, `ftCo_WarpStarFall_Coll`, `ftCo_800C4C60`, and `ftCo_WarpStarFall_Cam` from `asm/melee/ft/ft_0C31.s`.

Added C-side Warp Star fall callbacks in `ftCo_WarpStar.c`: empty anim/IASA functions, fall physics using `ftCommon_8007D494` and `ftCommon_8007D174`, collision handling through `ft_80083E64`, a fall-to-`JumpB` transition helper `ftCo_800C4C60`, and camera callback `ftCo_WarpStarFall_Cam` calling `ftCamera_800762F4`.
Generalized motion variable access from `fp->mv.co.warpstarjump` to `fp->mv.co.warpstar` for stored facing direction, timer `x1C`, saved position, self velocity, and ECB data.
Changed the Warp Star motion variable struct to store `ftCollisionBox ecb` instead of an untyped `x20`, and added a `jumpb` motion variable overlay with `x0`, `x4`, and float `x8` fields.
Typed several `ftCommonData` fields used by Warp Star movement as floats: `x438`, `x694`, `x698`, `x69C`, `x6A0`, `x6A4`, and `x6A8`.
Moved Warp Star fall prototypes from the generic `ft_0C31.h` header into the new `ftCo_WarpStar.h` header.
Improved helper prototypes: `ft_80083E64(Fighter_GObj* gobj, ftCollisionBox* ecb, HSD_GObjEvent cb)`, `it_802944AC(Item_GObj*, ftCollisionBox*)`, and `it_802947CC(Item_GObj* gobj, Vec3* pos)`.
smart_moves: Renaming the file/header from `WarpStarJump` to `WarpStar` matched the actual scope of the source after adding fall-state functions, avoiding a misleading module name once both jump and fall callbacks lived together.
Replacing untyped motion data with `ftCollisionBox ecb` made the collision callback `ftCo_WarpStarFall_Coll` express the real data flow: `ft_80083E64(gobj, &fp->mv.co.warpstar.ecb, ftCo_800C4C60)`.

The PR kept matching-oriented `ASM` functions with `#if !defined(MUST_MATCH) || defined(WIP)` C bodies and `#else` inline assembly fallbacks, allowing readable reconstruction while preserving exact assembly when required.
Typing `ftCommonData` offsets as floats allowed direct arithmetic in the physics reconstruction instead of opaque `UNK_T` casts or placeholder handling.

The `ftCo_800C4C60` C body mirrors the assembly at a behavior level by restoring facing direction, switching to `ftCo_MS_JumpB`, reinitializing jump motion variables, clamping horizontal velocity with `ABS`, updating the Warp Star item position, spawning effect `1030`, nudging camera event `4`, and calling `ftCommon_8007EBAC(fp, 14, 0)`.
Moving prototypes out of `ft_0C31.h` and into the feature-specific Warp Star header reduced reliance on a broad address-range header for now-decompiled functions.
decomp_lessons: When an action file grows beyond one specific substate, rename the module to the gameplay concept (`ftCo_WarpStar`) rather than keeping a narrower state name (`ftCo_WarpStarJump`).
Converting assembly often requires adjacent header/type cleanup: the real function signatures for item, collision, effect, and camera helpers made the C bodies practical.
Motion-variable unions should be named by shared state when multiple action states reuse the same saved data; here jump and fall both use `mv.co.warpstar` fields.
ECB-related unknown fields should be promoted to `ftCollisionBox` when the assembly passes the address to collision helpers; this improves both matching clarity and downstream type reuse.
Small no-op callbacks should be represented directly as empty C functions when their assembly is just `blr`.
For physics decompilation, identifying common-data offsets as floats can be the difference between opaque code and straightforward formulas involving stick input, acceleration, and velocity caps.
A partially matching helper can still unblock substantial asm deletion when wrapped with inline assembly fallback under the project’s `MUST_MATCH`/`WIP` pattern.
naming_conventions: `ftCo_WarpStar.c` / `ftCo_WarpStar.h` is preferred over `ftCo_WarpStarJump.*` once the file contains both `WarpStarJump` and `WarpStarFall` callbacks.
Common fighter motion variables use `fp->mv.co.<action>` naming; this PR changed `warpstarjump` to `warpstar` for shared Warp Star state.
Named struct member `ecb` replaced placeholder `x20` when the data is an `ftCollisionBox`.
Callback names preserve action-state suffixes: `_Anim`, `_IASA`, `_Phys`, `_Coll`, and `_Cam`.
Unidentified address-named static helper `ftCo_800C4C60` remained address-based because the PR evidence does not provide a semantic name.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Deleted 192 lines from `asm/melee/ft/ft_0C31.s` by replacing Warp Star fall assembly with C/ASM hybrid functions in `ftCo_WarpStar.c`.
Used `ASM void` functions with readable C under `#if !defined(MUST_MATCH) || defined(WIP)` and original inline assembly under `#else` to preserve exact matching paths.

For the fall physics match, the C reconstruction computes stick-scaled values from `p_ftCommonData->x69C`, selects signed `x6A0`, multiplies by `x6A4`, and calls `ftCommon_8007D174(fp, fp->self_vel.x, n2, n3, p_ftCommonData->x6A8)`.
For the collision callback match, the assembly branch target `.L_800C4C60` became a static C callback `ftCo_800C4C60`, passed by function pointer to `ft_80083E64`.
Used `ABS(n3)` and explicit negative/positive clamp branches in `ftCo_800C4C60` to model the assembly’s absolute-value comparison and sign-preserving velocity cap.
Retained local temporary blocks and simple `n0`/`n1`/`n2`/`n3` variable sequencing in C bodies, likely to influence evaluation order and register/float operation matching.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice, so there is no recorded reviewer feedback to extract.
follow_up_queries: Search later PRs for `ftCo_800C4C60` to see whether the 87.03% match was improved or the helper received a semantic name.
Search for later replacements of `mv.co.jumpb.x0`, `x4`, and `x8` with named jump-B motion fields.
Check whether `ftCommonData` fields `x438`, `x694`, `x698`, `x69C`, `x6A0`, `x6A4`, and `x6A8` were later named semantically.
Search for other item-rider actions that pass an `ftCollisionBox` through item helper APIs like `it_802944AC` and collision helper `ft_80083E64`.
